title: What would I do if I knew it would be easy?
subtitle: A journal set for examining decisions you are deferring because you expect them to be hard.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for Day 17 of the MONEY program. Five prompts for examining the financial decisions, actions, and asks you are avoiding because you have pre-decided they will be difficult.
sourceNotes: Drawn from Day 17 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and Week 3 of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when there is a financial action you have been putting off, or when you notice yourself assuming difficulty before you have tried.

content.text: A journal set for Day 17 of the MONEY program: examining the financial actions you are putting off because you have already decided they will be hard. Five prompts, around thirty minutes.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
Where in my money life am I currently bracing for difficulty?
Name the specific area, task, or conversation. Not a category — an actual thing.
When did I decide it would be hard?
Write the moment or the source. Was it a previous experience? Someone else's story? An assumption? A pattern?
What would I do this week if I knew it would be easy?
Write the list. The ask, the price, the conversation, the application, the transfer. What would you do if difficulty wasn't guaranteed?
What has already been easier than I expected, financially?
Two or three examples. Small counts. The point is the pattern.
What is the one thing from the "easy" list that I will do this week?
Name it. Then write down when — day, not "soon".
Drawn from Day 17 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and Week 3 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
